# NativeLink

> A high-performance remote build cache and execution platform implementing the
> Remote Execution API (Bazel, Buck2, Siso, Pants, BuildStream, CMake via recc).
> Open source, written in Rust.

NativeLink is four cooperating roles: content-addressable storage (CAS), the
action cache (AC), the scheduler, and workers. One binary runs any combination,
configured in JSON5. For a repository map and where-to-change-what, see
[AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md).

## Docs

- [Introduction](https://docs.nativelink.com): what NativeLink is and how to pick a starting point.
- [Getting started / Setup](https://docs.nativelink.com/getting-started/setup): run a local cluster.
- [Configuration](https://docs.nativelink.com/configuration/intro): the JSON5 schema and common shapes.
- [Configuration reference](https://docs.nativelink.com/reference/nativelink-config): every config field, autogenerated from the Rust crate.
- [Deployment](https://docs.nativelink.com/deployment/on-prem-overview): docker-compose, Kubernetes, and metrics.
- [Operate](https://docs.nativelink.com/operate): scaling the worker pool and the CAS, and running in production.
- [Explanations / Architecture](https://docs.nativelink.com/explanations/architecture): how the roles fit together.

## Source

- Repository: https://github.com/TraceMachina/nativelink
- Contributor guide: https://github.com/TraceMachina/nativelink/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
- Example configs: https://github.com/TraceMachina/nativelink/tree/main/nativelink-config/examples
- Runnable deployments: https://github.com/TraceMachina/nativelink/tree/main/deployment-examples
